items.title: The SpaceX factor: Hong Kong stocks face liquidity test from mega IPO
items.summary:
This is part of a series on the global impact of SpaceX’s historic IPO, tracing how mainland Chinese investors’ strategies, the Hong Kong market and wider capital flows are being reshaped by Elon Musk’s trillion‑dollar rocket gamble.
While the world witnesses a new fundraising record in the coming listing of SpaceX – and the emergence of its CEO Elon Musk as the first-ever trillionaire – Hong Kong

items.title: ‘Not a crackdown’: China regulators signal more neutral enforcement in shift from 2021
items.summary:
Chinese regulators are stepping up public enforcement against the nation’s corporate giants, marking a departure from the low-key approach adopted after a bruising 2021 tech crackdown.
In recent months, agencies have summoned company representatives, launched high-profile investigations, and named and shamed offenders.
However, while the uptick in activity has rattled investors, some analysts and

items.title: Meta reportedly begins dismantling $2 billion Manus deal on Beijing's orders
items.summary: Meta has reportedly begun dismantling its acquisition of Manus, moving to comply with Beijing's unprecedented order to hand back the Chinese-founded AI startup.

items.summary: The Nasdaq 100 could imminently include SpaceX, but first it’s getting a shakeup that will usher in another space-technology company and four flashy AI plays.

items.title: Pirro's losses in Fed investigation should stay on the books, judge rules
items.summary: In a scathing ruling, Judge James Boasberg denied U.S. Attorney for the District of Columbia Jeanine Pirro's request to vacate his earlier orders.

items.title: China’s BYD claims Germany’s plug-in hybrid crown, disrupting market of iconic auto giants
items.summary:
One out of every seven plug-in hybrid vehicles registered in Germany in May came from BYD, as the Chinese carmaker’s registrations surged 232 per cent in Europe’s largest auto market from a year earlier.
With 6,169 vehicles registered there in May, BYD held a 2.6 per cent share of Germany’s new-car registrations, according to data from the country’s Federal Motor Transport Authority.
Of those, 4,2

items.title: China Resources New Energy’s US$3.6 billion IPO smashes records on Shenzhen exchange
items.summary:
China Resources New Energy Holdings, the spin-off of a state-backed power producer, has smashed multiple records on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange, as it prepares to become the biggest initial public offering (IPO) and the first so-called red-chip company to trade on the bourse.
The wind and solar power producer, carved out of China Resources Power, planned to raise 24.5 billion yuan (US$3.6 billion)

items.summary: Oracle topped quarterly expectations, but free cash flow is negative, and the company plans to raise more capital for data center projects.

items.title: Global AI trade flashing signals reminiscent of the dotcom bust, analysts warn
items.summary:
The global artificial intelligence trade is flashing signals reminiscent of past market tops, with winners outpacing the broader market by a wide margin and a slew of jumbo initial public offerings (IPOs) in the pipeline.
The gap between richly valued stocks and cheaper ones had reached extremes seen only before the dotcom bust in March 2000, according to Bank of America.
